chapter_summary: Jesus goes to a wedding in Cana and turns water into wine — His first miracle. Then He travels to Jerusalem and clears the temple, saying it is His Father's house, not a marketplace.
main_lesson: Jesus shows His power and His love — He cares about the details of our lives AND about what is holy.

memory_verse.kjv: His mother saith unto the servants, Whatsoever he saith unto you, do it.
memory_verse.little_bible: Mary told the servants something very important: 'Whatever Jesus tells you to do — do it!' That is still great advice for us today!

parent_guide: This chapter has two big scenes that balance each other beautifully. At the wedding, Jesus shows tender care — He notices a family's embarrassment and acts quietly. At the temple, He shows holy anger — not out of selfishness, but because God's house was being treated carelessly. Help children see both sides: Jesus is gentle and powerful, kind and serious about what is right. The memory verse (v5) is one of the best short pieces of life-advice in the whole Bible.
application_for_children: When Jesus tells us to do something, we should do it — just like the servants did. We can trust Him because He loves us and knows what is best.

verses.kjv: And the third day there was a marriage in Cana of Galilee; and the mother of Jesus was there:
verses.little_bible: A few days later, there was a big wedding party in a town called Cana. Jesus' mother Mary was there.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: There was a wedding. Mary was there.
verses.meaning: Jesus and His mother attended a wedding celebration. This shows that Jesus took part in ordinary, joyful human events.
verses.prayer: Thank You, Jesus, for coming to be with people at happy times. You love celebrations too!
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been to a wedding? What was it like?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus went to parties and celebrations. What does that tell us about the kind of person He is?
verses.do_it_today: Draw a picture of a celebration Jesus would enjoy being part of.
verses.illustration_prompt: A cheerful outdoor wedding in Bible times, with tables of food, guests in robes, and a bright blue sky.

verses.kjv: And both Jesus was called, and his disciples, to the marriage.
verses.little_bible: Jesus and His friends, the disciples, were invited to the wedding too.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus and His friends came to the wedding.
verses.meaning: Jesus was not a loner. He went to social events with His friends and accepted invitations from people.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for being a friend who comes when He is invited. Help me always invite You into my day.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus said yes to the wedding invitation?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus spends time with people He loves. How can we invite Jesus into our family times?
verses.do_it_today: Before dinner tonight, say a short prayer inviting Jesus to be with your family.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus and a group of smiling disciples walking together toward a village celebration.

verses.kjv: And when they wanted wine, the mother of Jesus saith unto him, They have no wine.
verses.little_bible: During the party, the wine ran out. Mary went quietly to Jesus and said, 'They have no more wine.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The wine ran out. Mary told Jesus.
verses.meaning: Running out of wine at a wedding was a serious embarrassment in that culture. Mary brought the problem to Jesus — she knew He could help.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when something goes wrong, help me tell You about it just like Mary did. You care about my problems.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Mary went straight to Jesus when there was a problem?
verses.family_discussion: Mary trusted Jesus even before He had done any miracle. How can we bring our problems to Jesus first?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one problem in your life right now. Tell Jesus about it in prayer tonight.
verses.illustration_prompt: Mary speaking quietly and gently to Jesus at a crowded wedding feast, leaning close with a look of trust.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto her, Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come.
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered, 'Dear mother, why are you telling Me this? My time has not come yet.' But Mary was not worried.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said His time was not ready yet.
verses.meaning: Jesus' answer sounds strange to modern ears but was respectful. He was saying His public ministry had its own timing set by God. Yet He still acted — showing His love for His mother and for the family.
verses.memory_phrase: His hour was not yet come
verses.prayer: Lord, help me trust Your timing even when I don't understand it. You always do things at the right moment.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a time you had to wait for the right moment to do something?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus acts in His own perfect timing. How can we learn to trust God's timing rather than rushing Him?
verses.do_it_today: Practice patience today — let someone else go first in a game or at the table.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking calmly to Mary with a gentle expression; Mary listening with quiet confidence.

verses.kjv: His mother saith unto the servants, Whatsoever he saith unto you, do it.
verses.little_bible: Mary turned to the servants and said, 'Whatever Jesus tells you to do — do it!' She trusted Him completely.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Mary said: do whatever Jesus says.
verses.meaning: This is one of the most important instructions in the whole Bible. Mary's simple words — 'do whatever He says' — are the perfect summary of the life of faith.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to be like those servants. Whatever You say, I will do it. Help me obey You today.
verses.discussion_question: What is something Jesus has told us to do that we can obey today?
verses.family_discussion: Mary's advice is still the best advice for Christians today. What does 'doing whatever Jesus says' look like in your family?
verses.do_it_today: Find one thing Jesus teaches in the Bible and try to do it today on purpose.
verses.illustration_prompt: Mary speaking with confidence to attentive servants at a wedding banquet, pointing toward Jesus.

verses.kjv: And there were set there six waterpots of stone, after the manner of the purifying of the Jews, containing two or three firkins apiece.
verses.little_bible: Nearby stood six big stone jars used for washing — each one could hold a lot of water.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Six big stone jars stood nearby.
verses.meaning: The detail about six stone washing jars shows the setting clearly. These were large containers used for Jewish ceremonial washing — not drinking jars. Jesus was about to use something ordinary for something extraordinary.
verses.prayer: God, You use ordinary things to do amazing things. Thank You for using me too!
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of an ordinary object God might use to do something special?
verses.family_discussion: God often uses simple, everyday things to show His power. What ordinary things in your home could God use?
verses.do_it_today: Pick up an ordinary object at home and thank God that He can use ordinary things for His glory.
verses.illustration_prompt: Six tall stone water jars lined up against a courtyard wall, sunlight falling across them.

verses.kjv: Jesus saith unto them, Fill the waterpots with water. And they filled them up to the brim.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told the servants, 'Fill the jars with water.' They obeyed and filled every single jar to the very top.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said fill the jars. They filled them full!
verses.meaning: The servants obeyed completely — they filled the jars all the way to the brim. Full obedience brought the full miracle.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I want to obey You all the way, not just halfway. Help me fill up with Your words just like those jars.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the servants filled the jars all the way to the top?
verses.family_discussion: The servants obeyed fully, not partially. How does full obedience look different from half-hearted obedience?
verses.do_it_today: Do a chore today as fully and carefully as you can — all the way to the brim, not half-done.
verses.illustration_prompt: A servant pouring water from a large jug into a stone jar, filling it right to the rim; water glistening in sunlight.

verses.kjv: And he saith unto them, Draw out now, and bear unto the governor of the feast. And they bare it.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said, 'Now pour some out and take it to the man in charge of the feast.' They did exactly that.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said pour it out and take it. They did!
verses.meaning: The servants did not yet know what had happened — they simply obeyed each step. Obedience did not require them to understand the miracle first.
verses.memory_phrase: Take it to the man in charge
verses.prayer: God, help me obey You even when I don't fully understand. I trust that You know what You are doing.
verses.discussion_question: Do you think the servants knew the water had changed? How might they have felt?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes God asks us to take a step before we understand His plan. Can you think of a time that happened to someone in the Bible?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing today that someone in charge asks you to do without complaining — just like the servants.
verses.illustration_prompt: A servant carefully carrying a large cup of deep red wine toward a head table, eyes wide with wonder.

verses.kjv: When the ruler of the feast had tasted the water that was made wine, and knew not whence it was: (but the servants which drew the water knew;) the governor of the feast called the bridegroom,
verses.little_bible: The man in charge of the party tasted it — and it was wine! He did not know where it came from. But the servants who had filled the jars knew. The man called the groom over.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The man tasted it. It was wine! The servants knew the secret.
verses.meaning: The miracle was quiet and humble. Only the obedient servants witnessed the transformation. The person with authority didn't even know. Jesus did not need the credit.
verses.prayer: Lord, You do amazing things quietly. Help me notice Your miracles even when others miss them.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think only the servants knew what had happened?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus did not make a big announcement about His miracle. Why might He have kept it quiet? What does that teach us about humility?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind today without telling anyone. Do it as a secret gift.
verses.illustration_prompt: A surprised wedding master tasting wine from a goblet with raised eyebrows, while servants stand nearby with knowing smiles.

verses.kjv: And saith unto him, Every man at the beginning doth set forth good wine; and when men have well drunk, then that which is worse: but thou hast kept the good wine until now.
verses.little_bible: He said to the groom, 'People always serve the best wine first. But you saved the best for last!' He did not know it was Jesus' miracle.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The man said: you saved the best for last!
verses.meaning: God always gives His best. When Jesus provides, He does not give second-rate gifts. The best wine at the end is a picture of how God blesses beyond what we expect.
verses.prayer: Thank You, God, for always giving Your best. Help me be grateful for every good gift You give me.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of something in your life where God gave you something even better than you expected?
verses.family_discussion: God outdoes human generosity. How has God surprised your family with His goodness?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone in your family one thing you love about them — give them your best words.
verses.illustration_prompt: A joyful groom raising a goblet at a wedding feast, smiling with surprise and delight.

verses.kjv: This beginning of miracles did Jesus in Cana of Galilee, and manifested forth his glory: and his disciples believed on him.
verses.little_bible: This was the very first miracle Jesus did. It showed who He truly was — and His disciples believed in Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: This was Jesus' first miracle. His friends believed in Him!
verses.meaning: This first miracle was a sign — it revealed Jesus' glory and strengthened the faith of His disciples. Miracles are not just about fixing problems; they reveal who Jesus is.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I see the things You do, help my faith grow stronger every day.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think the disciples felt when they saw the water become wine?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus' miracles build our faith. What miracle of Jesus — from the Bible or your own life — has made you believe in Him more?
verses.do_it_today: Write or draw one thing about Jesus that makes you believe in Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus' disciples staring in amazement, one pointing at the stone jars, eyes wide with wonder and growing faith.

verses.kjv: After this he went down to Capernaum, he, and his mother, and his brethren, and his disciples: and they continued there not many days.
verses.little_bible: After the wedding, Jesus went with His mother, His brothers, and His disciples to a town called Capernaum. They did not stay long.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went to Capernaum with His family and friends.
verses.meaning: Jesus continued to live and travel with His family and closest followers. Even in His public ministry, family relationships were important.
verses.prayer: Thank You, Jesus, for caring about family. Help my family stay close to each other and close to You.
verses.discussion_question: Jesus traveled with His family and friends. Who do you love to be with?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus did not leave family behind when He started His work. How can your family travel together in faith?
verses.do_it_today: Do something together as a family today — a walk, a game, or a meal.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus walking with a small group — Mary, brothers, and disciples — along a dusty road near the Sea of Galilee.

verses.kjv: And the Jews' passover was at hand, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
verses.little_bible: It was almost time for the Passover — a very special celebration. So Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: It was Passover time. Jesus went to Jerusalem.
verses.meaning: The Passover was the most important Jewish festival, celebrating God rescuing Israel from Egypt. Jesus faithfully participated in His people's worship traditions.
verses.prayer: God, thank You for rescuing Your people. Thank You for sending Jesus to rescue me too.
verses.discussion_question: Do you know what Passover celebrates? (God rescued His people from Egypt!)
verses.family_discussion: The Passover pointed ahead to Jesus — the Lamb of God. How does knowing that make the Passover story even more special?
verses.do_it_today: Look up the Passover story in Exodus 12 and read a few verses together.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jerusalem on a hillside at golden hour, with travelers streaming up a wide road toward the city gates for a festival.

verses.kjv: And found in the temple those that sold oxen and sheep and doves, and the changers of money sitting:
verses.little_bible: When Jesus arrived at the temple, He found people selling animals and exchanging money right inside God's house.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: People were selling animals inside God's house.
verses.meaning: The temple courts had become a busy marketplace. What was meant to be a place of prayer and worship had become noisy and focused on money.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me keep my heart as a place where You are welcome — not full of distractions.
verses.discussion_question: How would you feel if someone turned your school or church into a loud marketplace?
verses.family_discussion: Our hearts are like a temple where God lives. What things can clutter our hearts and crowd God out?
verses.do_it_today: Before prayer tonight, sit quietly for one minute and let God into your heart without distractions.
verses.illustration_prompt: A busy, noisy temple courtyard crowded with animal pens, money tables, and bustling traders under stone archways.

verses.kjv: And when he had made a scourge of small cords, he drove them all out of the temple, and the sheep, and the oxen; and poured out the changers' money, and overthrew the tables;
verses.little_bible: Jesus made a whip out of cords. He drove out all the animals and the sellers. He flipped over the tables of the money changers and scattered their coins everywhere.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus drove out the sellers and flipped the tables!
verses.meaning: This was not a rage — it was holy action. Jesus deliberately made the whip, meaning He was purposeful, not impulsive. His anger was righteous, motivated by love for God's house.
verses.prayer: God, give me holy boldness — the courage to stand up for what is right, even when it is hard.
verses.discussion_question: Do you think Jesus was right to clear the temple? Why or why not?
verses.family_discussion: There is such a thing as righteous anger — being angry about things that truly dishonor God. How do we tell the difference from just being selfish or upset?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one thing at school or home that is not fair. Pray and ask God if He wants you to do something kind and brave about it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus, with a serious and determined expression, overturning a table as coins fly and animals scatter through a stone courtyard.

verses.kjv: And said unto them that sold doves, Take these things hence; make not my Father's house an house of merchandise.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said to the dove sellers, 'Take these things out of here! Do not turn my Father's house into a shop!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: do not make God's house a shop!
verses.meaning: Jesus called the temple 'My Father's house' — a clear statement of His identity. The temple was for worshipping God, not for making money.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You call God Your Father. Thank You that I can call Him my Father too. Help me honor Him wherever I am.
verses.discussion_question: What did Jesus mean when He said 'My Father's house'?
verses.family_discussion: When Jesus said 'My Father's house' He was saying He is God's Son. How does knowing Jesus is God's Son make His words more important to us?
verses.do_it_today: When you walk into church this week, take a moment to remember — this is God's house. Act like it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking with authority, arms outstretched, pointing out through the temple gates as doves flutter in the air.

verses.kjv: And his disciples remembered that it was written, The zeal of thine house hath eaten me up.
verses.little_bible: Jesus' disciples remembered a verse from the Psalms: 'My love for Your house burns like a fire inside me.' They understood — Jesus cared deeply about God's house.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The disciples remembered: Jesus loves God's house.
verses.meaning: The disciples recognized this as the fulfillment of Psalm 69:9. Jesus' actions matched what God's Word had already said. The disciples connected Scripture to what they were seeing.
verses.prayer: God, fill me with love for Your house and Your people. Help me care about the things You care about.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to be really passionate about something? What are you passionate about?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples connected what Jesus did to Scripture. How does knowing the Bible help us understand what God is doing today?
verses.do_it_today: Find one of your favorite Bible verses and write it on a piece of paper to put somewhere you can see it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two disciples standing together near a pillar, one opening a scroll, both with thoughtful, wondering expressions.

verses.kjv: Then answered the Jews and said unto him, What sign shewest thou unto us, seeing that thou doest these things?
verses.little_bible: Some of the religious leaders challenged Jesus: 'Show us a sign — prove to us that You have the right to do this!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The leaders said: prove that You have authority!
verses.meaning: The leaders wanted proof of Jesus' authority — a credential or sign. They were looking for evidence on their own terms, missing the sign that was standing right in front of them.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me not look for signs while missing You. Help me see You right in front of me.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the leaders asked for a sign instead of just believing what they saw?
verses.family_discussion: Sometimes people want proof before they believe. What is the difference between honest questions and stubborn unbelief?
verses.do_it_today: Ask one honest question about faith today. It is okay to ask — God can handle your questions.
verses.illustration_prompt: Religious leaders in official robes standing with arms crossed, confronting Jesus with stern expressions in the temple courts.

verses.kjv: Jesus answered and said unto them, Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered them with a mystery: 'Tear down this temple, and in three days I will build it back up.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: I will rebuild it in three days!
verses.meaning: Jesus was speaking about His own body — His death and resurrection. But the leaders heard it as a claim about the stone temple building. Jesus was giving them a sign pointing to Easter.
verses.memory_phrase: Three days I will raise it
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You rose from the dead in three days. Your resurrection is the greatest sign of all!
verses.discussion_question: What do you think Jesus really meant by 'this temple'?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus spoke in a way that pointed ahead to His resurrection. Why is the resurrection the most important thing that ever happened?
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud three times: 'Jesus died for me. Jesus rose for me. Jesus lives for me.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking to a group with calm authority, one hand raised; in the background, the grand temple columns tower above.

verses.kjv: Then said the Jews, Forty and six years was this temple in building, and wilt thou rear it up in three days?
verses.little_bible: The religious leaders scoffed: 'This temple took forty-six years to build — and You think You can raise it up in three days?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The leaders said: it took 46 years to build!
verses.meaning: The leaders misunderstood Jesus completely because they were thinking in physical terms. They could not imagine the deeper meaning — that He was talking about rising from the dead.
verses.prayer: God, open my heart and mind to understand Your Word. Help me not miss what You are really saying.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever misunderstood what someone said? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: The leaders thought Jesus was talking about stones and bricks. Why is it important to read the Bible carefully and ask what God really means?
verses.do_it_today: When you read the Bible this week, stop and ask: 'What is Jesus really teaching here?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Shocked and skeptical religious leaders, gesturing toward the grand stone temple in disbelief.

verses.kjv: But he spake of the temple of his body.
verses.little_bible: But Jesus was not talking about the stone building. He was talking about His own body — and how He would rise from the dead.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus meant His own body!
verses.meaning: John clarifies for the reader what Jesus really meant. This is one of Jesus' clearest predictions of His own resurrection, hidden in plain sight.
verses.prayer: Jesus, Your body died and rose again for me. That is the most amazing thing ever. Thank You.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it so amazing that Jesus said He would rise from the dead before He even died?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus knew what was coming — His death and resurrection. How does it change things to know that Jesus went to the cross on purpose, for us?
verses.do_it_today: Tell a friend or family member today: 'Did you know Jesus predicted He would rise from the dead?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A gentle close-up illustration of Jesus' face looking forward with calm peace, soft light surrounding Him.

verses.kjv: When therefore he was risen from the dead, his disciples remembered that he had said this unto them; and they believed the scripture, and the word which Jesus had said.
verses.little_bible: Later, after Jesus rose from the dead, His disciples remembered what He had said. Then they understood — and they believed God's Word and Jesus' words even more.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: After Jesus rose, His friends understood and believed!
verses.meaning: Faith often grows after the fact — the disciples understood Jesus' words better after the resurrection. Scripture and Jesus' words worked together to build their faith.
verses.prayer: God, as I grow up, help me understand more and more of what You have said. Keep reminding me of Your truth.
verses.discussion_question: Has there been a time you understood something in the Bible better after something happened to you?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples' faith grew through experiencing Jesus. How has experiencing God made your family's faith stronger?
verses.do_it_today: Think of something you have read in the Bible that you want to understand better. Ask God to show you.
verses.illustration_prompt: The disciples gathered together in morning light, one holding a scroll open, expressions of dawning wonder and joy.

verses.kjv: Now when he was in Jerusalem at the passover, in the feast day, many believed in his name, when they saw the miracles which he did.
verses.little_bible: While Jesus was in Jerusalem during the Passover feast, many people believed in Him because they saw the miracles He did.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Many people believed in Jesus after seeing His miracles.
verses.meaning: Miracles opened hearts to belief. But in the next verse, Jesus shows He knows the difference between genuine faith and being impressed by signs.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me believe in You not just because of miracles, but because I love You and trust You.
verses.discussion_question: Is believing because of miracles a good start? Can there be something even better than that?
verses.family_discussion: Seeing miracles can start someone's faith journey. But what keeps faith growing past just being impressed?
verses.do_it_today: Share one thing about Jesus — not a miracle, just who He is — that makes you love Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: A crowd in Jerusalem watching with wide eyes as Jesus heals someone; faces showing wonder, some with hands raised in praise.

verses.kjv: But Jesus did not commit himself unto them, because he knew all men,
verses.little_bible: But Jesus did not fully trust Himself to them. He knew what people are really like inside.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus knew what was in every person's heart.
verses.meaning: Jesus could see beneath the surface excitement. Some people followed for the spectacle, not genuine faith. Jesus, being God, knew all hearts perfectly.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You know everything about me — even the parts I hide. Thank You for loving me anyway.
verses.discussion_question: Is it a little scary or a little comforting that Jesus knows everything about you? Why?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus knows our hearts fully — the good and the mixed-up parts. How does His love for us despite that knowledge change how we feel about Him?
verses.do_it_today: Be honest with God in prayer today about one thing you have been hiding in your heart.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing quietly to one side of a crowd, looking out with knowing, gentle eyes as people celebrate around Him.

verses.kjv: And needed not that any should testify of man: for he knew what was in man.
verses.little_bible: He did not need anyone to tell Him what people were really like. He already knew what was in every human heart.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus already knew what was in every heart.
verses.meaning: This verse closes the chapter with a profound statement about Jesus' divine nature. He has perfect, complete knowledge of every person. Nothing is hidden from Him.
verses.memory_phrase: He knew what was in man
verses.prayer: Lord, You know my heart completely. Help me give You all of it — not just the good parts.
verses.discussion_question: If Jesus knows everything about you, what is the best way to respond to that?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus is fully God — He knows all things. How should that change the way we live and pray?
verses.do_it_today: Before you go to sleep tonight, open your heart to God in prayer and tell Him everything honestly.
verses.illustration_prompt: A warm illustration of a child kneeling in prayer, with a gentle light from above suggesting Jesus watching and knowing with love.
